entities.name: GenMine TOP Cancer Genome Profiling System
Okayama University Graduate School of Medicine, Dentistry, and Pharmaceutical Sciences
Comparative Analysis of a Dual DNA–RNA Panel and a DNA‐Only Panel for Sarcoma: Real‐World Data From a Nationwide Genomic Database

evidence_entities.text: The dual DNA–RNA test identified significantly more fusion genes (20.3% vs. 7.4%, p < 0.001) and therapeutically targetable kinase fusions (3.5% vs. 1.2%, p = 0.019) than the DNA‐only test.
Among patients with translocation‐related sarcomas, histology‐specific fusion genes were identified in 77.5% using the dual panel, compared to 40.0% with the DNA‐only panel (p < 0.001).
Additionally, 5.7% of patients had pathogenic germline variants identified through tumor–normal matched analysis.

In non‐gastrointestinal stromal tumor sarcomas, the dual test showed a trend toward higher rates of genotype‐matched therapy (4.3% vs. 2.6%, p = 0.25) and a significantly higher rate of molecular targeted therapy (4.3% vs. 1.5%, p = 0.03).

notes: This paper compares two cancer genomic profiling tests (GenMine TOP vs FoundationOne CDx) in sarcoma patients using real-world data from Japan's nationwide database.
The dual DNA-RNA panel (GenMine TOP) showed superior performance in detecting fusion genes and kinase fusions compared to the DNA-only panel.
The study included 1046 patients with various sarcoma subtypes and demonstrated the clinical utility of comprehensive genomic profiling in sarcoma management.
